frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer box visible with peg board, right-hand grasper positioned near pegs, left grasper ready. Monitor displays pattern feedback.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment setup appears standard. Bimanual setup suggests right-hand dominant trainee.

frame_analyses.description: Left (non-dominant) grasper approaching first peg to grasp initial object. Right grasper positioned in ready stance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth approach, grasper angle appropriate for grasp.

frame_analyses.description: First object being transferred through midair space between pegs. Both graspers visible managing the object.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled transfer, no instrument clash visible.

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper placing first object onto destination peg. Object appears securely positioned.
Left grasper continues picking up subsequent objects. Multiple pegs visible with remaining objects.
Mid-sequence phase showing alternating transfers. Trainer shows desk layout with supplies and monitor feedback.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Performance appears competent. No visible drops or major fumbles in visible frames.

frame_analyses.description: Trainer moves box position slightly, suggesting transition to reverse transfer phase. Objects appear to be repositioned on pegs.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup change indicates phase transition per protocol.

frame_analyses.description: Reverse phase initiated. Right grasper (now non-dominant in reverse) picking up objects to return to original positions.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed as protocol requires.

frame_analyses.description: Objects being transferred back via midair in reverse sequence. Grasper coordination maintained.
Final objects being placed in completion phase. Trainer box shows near-empty state on reverse side, indicating advanced progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Movement efficiency appears good. No instruments dropped visible in final frames.

task_specific_assessments.transfer_quality: No visible drops or placement failures in available frames
task_specific_assessments.hand_coordination: Smooth bimanual coordination throughout visible sequence
task_specific_assessments.object_placement: All visible placements appear secure on pegs
task_specific_assessments.phase_execution: Protocol phases (forward and reverse) observed and executed

confidence_rationale: Video shows setup, desk environment, and overhead camera angles of FLS trainer throughout sequential operations. However, the video does NOT show a continuous recording of a complete performance with clear start/stop timing. Frames are sparse overhead shots mixed with equipment setup views. No visible timer or timestamp overlay. Cannot determine actual completion time, total penalties, or final score. Video appears to be instructional demonstration of FLS peg transfer task setup and technique rather than a timed performance recording.
cannot_determine: Exact completion time — no timer or final timestamp visible in frames
Whether any objects were dropped outside field of view (overhead angle may obscure drops)
Exact sequence of all 6 objects transferred (only partial visibility in frames provided)
Final object placement confirmation — video ends before clear completion signal
Whether all objects reached correct pegs or remained on incorrect pegs
classification_rationale: This video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL rather than PERFORMANCE because: (1) Multiple frames show desk setup, equipment arrangement, and trainer box positioning from overhead/external angle rather than trainee point-of-view; (2) Frames appear to be taken at intervals showing demonstration or setup rather than continuous timed recording; (3) No on-screen timer, start/stop signal, or standardized FLS documentation visible; (4) Camera angles suggest documentation or teaching context rather than proctored performance recording.

demonstrated_techniques: Bimanual grasper coordination for object transfer
Smooth mid-air object handoff between instruments
Hand-switching protocol for reverse phase execution
Forward and reverse phase sequencing per FLS standard
common_errors_addressed: Maintaining secure grasp at 2/3 point of object
Executing clean hand switches between forward and reverse phases
Preventing instrument collision during bimanual transfer

improvement_suggestions: Faster object pickup and placement would reduce transfer phase time toward expert benchmark (40-60s total)
More deliberate reverse phase transitions could improve efficiency
